# Transcrição: How_To_Change_Your_Communication_Style_Without_Judgement_From_Others!

- **Fonte:** How_To_Change_Your_Communication_Style_Without_Judgement_From_Others!
- **Duração:** 01:57
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:57]

I have this question regarding how do we switch the way we communicate? I feel that I overly identify with a certain type of voice that is serious, more academic, more teacher like, it makes me feel like I'm not being myself and people will find out that this is not who I am. How do you recommend that I break this mold that I have crafted over years that you know, this is why I want to identify myself with? Thank you for your question. The easiest way to be able to explore different ways of communicating, different sides of who we are, is in new environments. It's really difficult to explore a different version of you in an environment that already has lots of preconceived ideas of exactly how you are and how you communicate. Because when you try to break out of that mold in an environment that has a mold for you, sometimes internally we hold ourselves back, but on top of that, sometimes externally, the external forces of the environment hold us back too. Because the moment you try to speak differently, people might just look at you a little bit weird and that look is all it takes for you to go back to what you normally do. So I would look for new environments. I would look for environments where people would have no idea who I am. I'll try to find different interests. I'll try to find different hobbies. I probably wouldn't do this because I don't particularly enjoy this, but again, if I desperately needed a new environment, I might go to a baking class. I might go to a baking class and I'll go to that class and I'll try a different version of it, because no one has an idea who Vin is there. And I'll try a different version of me, because when I try a different version of me there, no one's going to look at me weird. Because they actually have no context on who I am. So whoever I show up as, they will immediately accept me as that person, which gives me time to practice this other version, this other side of me that I want to be able to try out. I call that neutral ears. And again, a neutral ear is someone who has no preconceived idea of who you are, what you sound like and how you communicate. Don't underestimate the value of new environments, because they allow us to explore different sides of who we are, because there's no pressure and there's no preconceived idea, there's no prejudice, there's nothing.
